title: Whitney Wolfe Herd and Sophia Kianni at Solve At MIT 2023
text:
[Music]
hi everyone
hi everyone I'm so excited to be doing
this today with Whitney who is a hero
and an incredible Mentor for me and so
I'd love to start out first with of
course you next year will Mark a decade
since you first launched Bumble in 2014
and love and connection are two things
that most Define what makes us Cuban and
bubble has expanded from love binding to
friend finding so how do you see
Technology's evolving role in building
connection and how do you mitigate the
dangers of Outsourcing love and
friendship to the algorithm yeah so I
can't believe it will be almost a decade
I think it's important to kind of go
back in time a little bit to understand
how we approached solving
for what was broken in love so you know
if you look back over the course of
history love has always operated a
certain way it is something that's
actually been quite toxic you know if
you go back to even the prehistoric
times women were captured and then you
fast forward to Medieval Times and you
know there was lots of Dowry and there's
just been this undercurrent of
toxic love where women were essentially
forgotten through the course of love and
then Modern Love didn't operate all that
differently right of course you know
there were some advances but I grew up
in the era of the rules where women were
meant to be quiet you know it meant um a
man liked you if he was mean to you and
so there was this toxic abusive
ecosystem around offline love and then
love went digital
and no one ever sat and changed any of
the rules of love but what also happened
was there was this intersection of the
toxic internet so if you look at the way
the internet was actually set up there
was no guard rails for how we treat each
other or how we engineer a Kinder
ecosystem or an environment to be more
polite you know you drive down the road
you see signs that say see something say
something there's nothing like that on
the internet right and it's just the
Wild Wild West and so what happened at
the explosion of online dating was all
of the toxic broken mechanics of Love
offline
uh really intersected with the dangers
of the internet online so this was this
moment to take a step back and say what
if there's a way to do this differently
what if we could actually use technology
to be a force for positivity it doesn't
have to be toxic it doesn't have to be
the way it always was and so Bumble was
really about rewriting the rules not
just of love and not just of how women
are treated but of how we actually
behave on the internet so that we can
have healthier offline love so I think
it's it's a big responsibility of a lot
of the folks out there with AI right now
to not make the same mistake again how
do we actually think about women at the
intersection of this next kind of
digital wave and so you know I think
this is where technology leaders have to
step in and have guard rails and ground
rules we do not tolerate hate we will
ban someone if they're on the app and
we've taken it offline with legislation
we passed a law in several States now
that actually makes sending an
unsolicited lewd image illegal so this
has to be an effort Beyond just
technology as well
so
moving to you
um I have to just take a moment to say
how remarkable this young woman is I
mean the fact that she is 21 years old
and on the stage here and also balancing
school and being a you know a young
woman I just I'm so impressed with you
and you set such an amazing example for
all the young entrepreneurs and
activists around the world so thank you
for being you gives me great hope for my
children and everyone ahead so Sophia
you started a non-profit as a teenager
after really witnessing the devastating
effect that pollution was having on your
parents home country of Iran and through
climate Cardinals you're using
technology to make climate education
more accessible by translating this
scientific literature into more
languages so three years in which is
remarkable how do you see Technology's
role in really educating people about

disinformation this is something that is
such an issue about the climate crisis
in your work
yeah so it's so exciting for me because
when climate Cardinals first started we
were entirely youth-led youth run and
volunteer oriented so on the very first
day we launched we had over a thousand
young people sign up to volunteer with
us with an average age of just 16 and
together they were able to translate
over half a million words of climate
resources into over a hundred languages
and a few months ago we were very
fortunate to create a partnership with
Google and we now are using their uh
latest and greatest translation
technology to basically automate our
translation process at a very very high
efficacy rate and so we've now been able
to translate over a million words of
climate Resources with their health
which has been truly incredible and it
in my opinion also really showcases not
just the power of technology to be used
for good but also the power of
intergenerational cooperation of a
private sector and a public sector and a
private private sector and a non-profit
that coming together to actually come up
with a solution that helps people so in
my mind it was really like a win-win-win
and of course being at Stanford I just
have seen how AI has become the new
buzzword and I know it's something that
I've seen in all of my classes and I'm
really excited to continue to to think
about how can we leverage technology to
further social impact of course this
what we've done with climate Cardinals
is just the start and we're really
excited to continue to grow and expand
this partnership with Google and to
explore other ones but for me also I
mean my next step as you know is going
to be starting a company with my best
friend Phoebe at Stanford and we
similarly are really excited to explore
how can we use technology to make it
easier to shop sustainable fashion
Solutions so I think for all young
people I really encourage to look at how
can we leverage them how can we leverage
Tech AI like this is what is going to be
the future and there are so many ways
that we can integrate that with honestly
The Passion of young people to get
things done to make a difference to make
our results even greater I think that's
incredible and just to further that for
a second I was really Blown Away to hear
the way you were talking about this
generation of peers that are so
passionate about climate change yet they
might still be shopping at you know very
toxic
um you know places for the environment
so they're buying fast fashion because
that's all they have access to maybe in
their mind and so I think this new
company that you're starting is
phenomenal in the sense that you
actually make it tangible and easier
because when you think how do I go buy
sustainable clothing this is a daunting
question how do you even navigate that
right you'd have to spend your whole day
going around town so I think that you
know making that easier will be an
extension of what you've already done
with with your non-profit and in just
educating and I think that is where it
all starts and what about the the
misinformation piece how do you combat
that
I completely agree with you and I think
actually it all Harbors back to
education climate Cardinals is an
education non-profit we're trying to
make it uh as accessible as possible for
people to learn about climate change by
translating into over a hundred
languages and similarly with this new
company really what we're trying to make
is is create this movement for people to
be more educated about the ills of fast
fashion because of course young people
are also the most likely to say they
care about climate change but then
they're also the most likely to go and
correct and Shop from these fast fashion
companies and so it really is a matter
of making sure that they feel empowered
that they understand really what impact
their dollar is having and how they can
use it for good and so in my opinion
like one of the biggest ways that we can
fight climate misinformation is by
putting quality educational resources
out there in a way that is accessible
and digestible for people I'll give an
example the UN has an ipcc report it's
their uh report that basically
summarizes the current state of the
climate crisis it's very long very
technical very scientific and it's
typically only available in the six un
languages that account for less than
half of the world's speaking population
and so what climate Cardinals did for
the most recent ipcc synthesis is we
worked with a group of scientists and we
actually created an executive summary of
the ipcc synthesis and then we
translated that into Over 20 Languages
and then we use social media to
disseminate it to hundreds of thousands
of people and that to me is like the
perfect example of the way that we fight
the negativity of the internet is by
putting more positivity out there I
agree with that 100 and I think that's a
perfect segue into talking about how
harassment and bullying are real online
and women take the brunt of it I know
for me I have increasingly become more
active on social media and it honestly
has come to the detriment of my mental
health because abuse is so real and so
how do you engineer better behavior and
better mental health outcomes on your
app and others well I'm I hate that
that's happened to to you and candidly
that's what sparked Bumble for me when I
left Tinder the internet what it felt
like was just attacking me it was
happening at 10 pm it was happening at
10 A.M no matter where you were and I
had this moment where I said wow the
youth is in crisis because bullying in
my era was on the playground but then
you'd go home and you were safe for that
time right and it didn't make it better
but the way we are just married to our
phones at this point and the comments
the playground is now 11 p.m on your
cell phone while you're laying in bed I
mean it's devastating and you know
according to the U.S Surgeon General
really actually on the second was when
it was posted of this month we're in a
crisis of loneliness and a lot of that
is the disconnection The Bullying the
abuse from the internet so I think what
we've set out to do and this was the
founding mission was if toxicity can be
viral
so can positivity so can kindness right
there's always the other side of that
coin and so I think there is a big
responsibility on the big social media
platforms but what we're doing and what
I'm proud of at Bumble is we are a very
rare technology that's actually focused
on connecting you and that sounds
counter-intuitive because you're like
okay all these connection tools they're
very disconnecting right sitting on your
phone in isolation and just watching
people and having these you know kind of
disconnected conversations that's not
true connection that's not relationships
so we're in the quest of actually
getting you to a real relationship
through healthier engagement on the
internet and that means if you abuse
you're gone you're off if you harass or
you use hate speech you're out and this
is where AI gets to be very interesting
for us so we've already been leveraging
AI for years to detect hate and to be
predictive in a positive way as well
like this person is a great person let's
Elevate them to the top right there
behavior is great and there's these
triggers you can look for with with
these algorithms and so I think we are
not perfect right we have a long way to
go technology is a tricky thing because
you do one thing good it leads to
another bad thing and you know it's a
never-ending road but we're dedicated to
it like that our tagline is kind
connections and so I think we need more
technology platforms at scale I'm not
talking about you know the small tiny
startup out there that's trying to do
good and they we need more of those
because they become the big ones but we
also need leaders at the biggest
platforms to take a more aggressive
stance on Behavior because to your point
going on social media the flip side of
his being abused and we cannot allow
that anymore and I think you know this
is this is why I get up in the morning
is to try and combat this
so on that point
um last year
um oh wait where okay did we go ahead
sorry let's see
um I will hand it over to you yes

so how do you think about these issues

yeah so the the stats are Stark right

so we have to prioritize
um you know making sure that we open

the battles I've been on just to get to

was even more
um resourced than so many other women
right I'd come from Tinder I had a

have to actively open doors and we have
to actively advocate so anyone that is
in a role that has a voice that has
impact you know when you get that DM

know really take it to heart and
consider opening a door and so I think
it is it is on the funders of course but
we're not going to sit in here and talk

we all know the Dynamics there but I

like you to really spend that time to

um we can't keep it all for ourselves
it's it's really about paying it forward
but
um I think it's it's absolutely
um a crisis and it's something we have

but I'm so proud of you and I think it's

um but I'm so honored to have spent a

bye
